name: The Legal Side of Open Source
description: Everything you've ever wondered about the legal side of open source, and a few things you didn't.
author: GitHub
learningObjectives:
After this lesson, the learner should be able to:
- Explain the legal implications of publishing code publicly on GitHub
- Select an appropriate open source license for a project
- Identify when a contributor license agreement (CLA) is needed
- Describe what a company's legal team needs to know about open source
abstract: A comprehensive guide to the legal dimensions of open source for maintainers, contributors, and institutional stakeholders. Covers licensing choice, contributor agreements, copyright, CLA requirements, and organizational compliance considerations.
audience: Maintainers, Project Leads, OSPO Staff, Legal Teams
notes: Pairs well with Understanding Software Licensing for a complete licensing track
teaches: Navigate the legal aspects of open source licensing, contribution agreements, and compliance
